copy.title: Angels stun Dodgers in LA. Spurs-Knicks Game 3 tonight. Tech selling hard.
copy.keyTakeaway: Tech stocks got hammered today—QQQ down nearly 5%—while the Spurs-Knicks NBA Finals tip off tonight. Baseball saw some lopsided scores out West, and Kimi Antonelli won Monaco.

todaysHits.sports: Angels shock Dodgers 13-5; Spurs-Knicks Finals Game 3 tonight.
todaysHits.f1: Kimi Antonelli wins Monaco; Hamilton second.
todaysHits.culture: Tony Awards tonight; Masters of Universe streaming release looms.

lead.headline: Angels blast Dodgers 13-5, steal the division spotlight
lead.whatHappened: Los Angeles Angels dominated the Dodgers 13-5, a statement win that flips the script in Southern California. The Angels' offense came alive while the Dodgers couldn't keep pace.
lead.whyBullet1: Division rivals don't forget nights like this—the Angels proved they're capable of taking down LA's marquee team.
lead.whyBullet2: The Dodgers' defense and pitching both cratered in the same game, suggesting deeper problems than just a bad night.
lead.whatToSay: The Angels just reminded everyone they're in the same division conversation. That's the kind of win you bring up when people sleep on you.

sportsOther: Angels embarrassed the Dodgers at home. That doesn't happen often.
Brewers demolished Colorado like they were playing a different sport.
Phillies handled the White Sox with ease, nothing dramatic.

markets.mood: Tech stocks sold off hard today — SPY dropped 2.6% and the Nasdaq fell 4.8% — as the 10-year Treasury yield climbed 1.3 percent and signaled rising rate expectations.
markets.whyBullet1: When Treasury yields spike, growth stocks get punished because their future earnings are worth less in today's dollars. NVDA, TSLA, and DELL each dropped over 6%, which tells you the market is repricing what expensive tech is actually worth. Meanwhile AAPL held up better at down 1.2%, suggesting defensive positioning.
markets.whyBullet2: Friday's jobs report will either confirm the Fed's narrative that the economy is cooling or prove that inflation pressure is still real. If unemployment ticks up, this selling gets validated. If job growth stays hot, you're looking at another round.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin climbed 1.6% while everything else cratered — that's the classic flight-to-alternative-assets move when people stop trusting where rates are headed. Not a sign of confidence in tech, more a sign people are hedging their bets.

golf.headline: Poston wins Memorial in playoff over Gerard at Muirfield
golf.whyCare1: Memorial is the last tune-up before the US Open, and Poston just proved he can win on a brutally difficult course.
golf.whyCare2: Muirfield Village is Jack Nicklaus's course and plays like a US Open — if you can win here, you can win anywhere.
golf.watchFor: Whether Poston carries this momentum to Pinehurst for the US Open in two weeks.
golf.whatToSay: Poston just beat a good field on the hardest course of the year. That's the kind of thing that matters in majors.

f1.headline: Antonelli wins Monaco in Mercedes statement
f1.whyCare1: Kimi Antonelli took the street circuit's most prestigious race, signaling Mercedes' resurgence in a tight season.
f1.whyCare2: Hamilton finished second, keeping his championship hopes alive despite the early-season gap to the leaders.
f1.watchFor: Whether Antonelli can convert Monaco success into consistency at circuits that don't reward raw pace.
f1.whatToSay: Antonelli finally broke through at the one track where it matters most. Mercedes has something real this year.

culture.topic: Marner's hat trick puts Vegas up in Cup Final
culture.whatHappened: Marner scored the fastest hat trick in Stanley Cup Final history to lead Vegas to a Game 3 win.
culture.whyItMatters: Vegas is one win away from hoisting the Cup, and Marner just showed up exactly when it matters most.
culture.whatToSay: That's the kind of performance that gets your name on the Cup. Marner's been waiting for this.

culture.topic: Tony Awards celebrate monsters, vampires, and actual talent
culture.whatHappened: The Tony Awards happened with literary monsters and vampires as part of the night's theme and cake moments.
culture.whyItMatters: It's the one awards show where people actually care about the work instead of just the red carpet.
culture.whatToSay: Did you catch the Tonys? Actually worth watching this year.

culture.whatHappened: Robert De Niro as a former president called back in to investigate a massive cyberattack on American infrastructure.

copy.finalSharpTake: Tech got crushed today—SPY down 2.6%, QQQ down 4.8%—and the bond market's screaming that traders think something's broken in the economy. Meanwhile Bitcoin's up 1.6%, which means money's looking for somewhere safe that isn't a stock. Tonight's Spurs-Knicks Game 3 matters only if you're a basketball guy; what matters is whether this selling continues or snaps back tomorrow.
